---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Results

## Model Development

This section should explain the stepwise development from the starting model to
the selected diagnostic or base model, with emphasis on changes that materially
alter key quantities such as depletion, recruitment, and fishing mortality.

The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment used a detailed stepwise progression
to separate changes in model structure, input data, CPUE treatment, biological
assumptions, tagging treatment, and convergence procedures. The same reporting
principle should be used here: each retained development step should have a
short purpose, a reproducible model label, and a clear statement of its effect
on key derived quantities.

> **TODO:** Add the final `r assessment_year` stepwise model-development
> narrative and figures. Do not reuse the `r previous_assessment_year` stepwise
> outcomes unless the corresponding models have been rerun or explicitly
> retained.

## Model Diagnostics

Diagnostics should cover objective function behaviour, gradients, Hessian
status, jitter outcomes, boundary hits, retrospective or profile diagnostics
where available, and any data conflicts identified during model development.

> **TODO:** Summarise diagnostics from the final selected model set. Do not mark
> a model as successful if a required MFCL or diagnostics check failed.

## Fits to Data

The report should present fits to CPUE, size composition, tagging data, and any
conditional age-at-length data used in the model.

> **TODO:** Add final fit summaries and reference the corresponding figures.

## Model Estimates

This section should present selectivity, movement, natural mortality, growth,
recruitment, spawning potential, depletion, fishing mortality, and other key
derived quantities.

Report-ready figures are inserted in the Figures section when the report is
rendered from selected analysis outputs. The key-quantity figure should use
the same accepted plotting definitions as the interactive review figures, then
be saved at report resolution.

The `r assessment_year` results should be compared against the
`r previous_assessment_year` baseline only after equivalent quantities have
been generated by the current workflow. The previous assessment reported median
recent spawning depletion of 0.35 for 2018-2021, with an 80 percent interval
of 0.30-0.40 and a full range of 0.25-0.46 after combining structural and
estimation uncertainty. It reported median $F_{recent}/F_{MSY}$ of 0.59 for
2017-2020, with an 80 percent interval of 0.46-0.74 and a full range of
0.37-0.99.

| Quantity | `r previous_assessment_year` value | `r assessment_year` value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Recent spawning depletion | 0.35 for 2018-2021; 80 percent interval 0.30-0.40; full range 0.25-0.46 | TODO |
| Recent fishing mortality relative to $F_{MSY}$ | 0.59 for 2017-2020; 80 percent interval 0.46-0.74; full range 0.37-0.99 | TODO |
| Limit reference point status | No uncertainty-grid model, including estimation uncertainty, below 20 percent $SB_{F=0}$ | TODO |
| Overall stock-status conclusion | Not overfished and not undergoing overfishing | TODO |

These previous values are included to guide comparisons and should not be used
as `r assessment_year` stock-status advice.

## Sensitivities and Structural Uncertainty

Sensitivity runs should identify which data or assumptions most affect stock
status and management quantities. The structural uncertainty grid should then
summarise the agreed axes and levels used for management advice.

In the previous assessment, the largest stock-status effects in the structural
uncertainty grid were associated with tag mixing period, stock-recruitment
steepness, and size-composition weighting. Other one-off sensitivities
addressed data and model assumptions such as tag treatment, size-data
weighting, age-data weighting, selectivity, and biological inputs. The current
results should identify whether the same assumptions remain influential and
whether new data or model settings change their ranking.

> **TODO:** Add final sensitivity and grid interpretation once the agreed model
> set is complete.

## Stock Assessment Results

The final stock-status section should report recent depletion for
`r recent_period`, fishing mortality relative to reference levels, uncertainty
ranges, and WCPFC reference-point interpretation.

> **TODO:** Insert final management-advice values from the structural
> uncertainty grid and estimation-uncertainty pipeline.
